author: VermDragon Fmr- Head of Econ SAMUD
content: A deep thought I had today.  Things I remember well- Why do you only get 140 characters, that's dumb, why do you need the internet and electronic mail, that seems unnecessary, Amazon has lost money for 10 years straight-why would you invest in a losing business.  Why would Netflix stop sending me DVD's and start streaming everything-I have 56K lines.  That bitcoin sounds like a scam and people are all gonna get hacked-scam!   And along those lines last/this year-NFT's those seem dumb and it is a scam.  Why would I rent somebody's house instead of a hotel-that will never work.   Why would games need NFT's, they are way behind on their roadmap and people are gonna lose their money- Scam.     History doesn't repeat itself-it stutters.  These are stutters of the fearful and the uneducated that I witnessed for myself and these fears caused me to lose millions of opportunities that I could have been early.   My point is educate yourself and watch for the "smart" people who make you believe that new and advancements are scams when they themselves just don't get it.  SA to me is a combination of so many great technologies that are ground breaking and fun, but also the opportunity to change the way that folks can get ahead in the world.  During a trying time in much of the world, comes the Star Atlas team and with them the dreams of so many.  If I'm going to possibly lose money, I want to risk that money on a team that believes in helping the world with tech and new ideas, like the Star Atlas team.  Welcome to what is next.

author: [HEIM] Dinarei | Vaelis.bod
content: I couldn’t attend the TH, I read the new article about the risk zones. It looks like the word “insurance” is totally misused. What they describe is not insurance. It’s a sort of fixed fee to play in HRZ but I don’t see how you can call that insurance. The insurance part doesn’t have any cost if they don’t actually have to pay for something that got lost. Any details shared about that?

author: [HEIM] Dinarei | Vaelis.bod
content: I’m talking about the other part, the “insurance company”, per say. They’re not offering a repayment or anything. It has no cost on their side if insured ships can’t even be looted. A real insurance is when the insurance company actually endorses part of the risk on their side. In this case they have zero risk since they just remove the risk alltogether by saying an insured ship can’t be looted. They make it look like an insurance but really it doesn’t look like it is. The cost is on the pirztes and pvp players who’ll lose profit and not on the insurance company that will be the winner in all cases.

reply.preview: Original message was deleted

author: Quimera | JohnKeel ⪛⦿⫺ONI
content:
I have my doubts about the insurance mechanic too but i dont understand why some people seems soo against it already just a few hours after it was announced considering all things are subject to change and adapt, i assume this is the first draft of the mechanic and we've known for some time a feature like this was in the minds of the team.
Surely if it wasnt implemented by the team it would have been from a 3d party in some other way  and its still possible to happen with lower prices and different mechanics.

author: Quimera | JohnKeel ⪛⦿⫺ONI
content:
Also ive noticed some complaining the most are the pirate based guilds because (I assume) they feel like there wont be any loot for them to take on the medium risk zone and few in the high risk zone if everyone is insured.
As the mechanics show right now, cargo recently collected cant be insured until reaching a safe zone or such, so there is profit to be made out of miners/gatherers and also not everyone will take an insurance or not everything will be insured.
Adding to it, EXP will be acquired no matter what and im pretty sure it will be really important to bolster your fleets, crafting paths, etc

author: Quimera | JohnKeel ⪛⦿⫺ONI
content:
And finally i've heard a couple of times leaders of pirates guilds comenting on twitter spaces or Atlas brews (not sure if it was from Skull&Bones or Dark) they would like a broadcasting system to allow enemies to surrender instead of fight and get off for a fee or even pay a regular fee to be able to move undisturbed (always reminded me of the thieves guild from discworld).
Not sure how far this idea is from an insurance system, in the end piracy is just a word

author: Quimera | JohnKeel ⪛⦿⫺ONI
content: and I understand nobody will be forced to purchase an insurance, the combat stance mechanic and medium risk zone surely will give enough oportunities for dedicated players to make profit and fun without having to pay for an insurance but there are always risks ofc (I always thought the pve on SA would be more challenging than the PVP because few people would want to risk their assets, with insurances it may change my perspective)

author: [IA] DougEFresh1417 | BULKMUD
content: yeah the whole insurance thing i don't think was thought out carefully by them.  Even Michael mentioned they are still working through it .  There are a lot more questions that are raised by that.. I think in the MRZ everything should be consented... IE you want to battle  ( do you want to risk assets)  all has to be signed off by both parties via a smart contract.   The HRZ should be free for all and insurance should come from guild and DAC's   not via SA in my opinion

author: [IA] DougEFresh1417 | BULKMUD
content: Secondly with regards to  (  teaming up with a friend to fraud the insurance company)  it wont work like that..  When you blow up a ship in the HRZ  ( you don't get all the components )  example the person that blew up the ship claims the loot and parts. Which then entitles them to build that ship  with 35% of the funds  of mkt price.   The insurance policy then covers the person who lost the ship with 65% coverage...   So in the end the ship was just split in terms of value.. btw the two players..  No one benefits

author: [IA] DougEFresh1417 | BULKMUD
content: my guess is it will be closer to 95% split so the insurance company makes some vig plus fees incurred for the policy

content: I'm with you on this. Additionally, it is premature to flip out over insurance without even knowing it's cost. Like everything else it will take some iterations to balance this mechanic to satisfy both peaceful builder/entrepreneur player and the warmonger pirate. What's clear to me is that insurance is absolutely needed in game where your assets may be worth 10s or 100s of thousands $, so I am fully supportive of it.  I think insurance premiums and deductibles should be collected by the SA DAO for POLIS holders to vote on what to do with the funds.

author: Aleister Crowley
content:
I have a marketing/advertising background and well, there are 2 ways of seeing this:

-Perspective of those that understand the process, and that we are in for the long haul seeing that getting in early will reap rewards. (Most cases folks with biggest wallets)
vs
-Those that want to get in a ship now and fly away to the sunset and until that happens will yell FIRE every time they have a chance. 

So SA from a marketing perspective has to work closely with exchange sites, guilds, community managers and leaders to educate both sides of the player base, to identify weaknesses in the message conveyed and also strengths to be followed. Town Halls to me are awesome and I love hearing from the team but again, there are folks that want a ship they can interact with, and when they get that ship they want weapons that work, and after the weapons they want customizable suits ... And so on and so forth.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
[AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content:
Let's say I pay $250 to insure my $1000 ship for 100%.

Now my friend comes along and blows me up. I get $1000 back, but what does my friend get in salvage? The answer is: at most $250, because that is the only way to make sure value does not get created through this encounter. If it would be more than $250, I could do this non-stop as it would net result in profit for the two of us.

The balance would look like this:
Me: -$250 - $1000 (ship) + $1000
Friend: +$250
Net: 0

Is that cool for actual pirates? No, not cool at all. But it's the reality of a system like that unfortunately 

How can we make the best of this for pirates:
- make fees high
- make max insurable value lower than 100% (e.g. max 80%)

How can we make the best of this for everyone players wishing to play in HRZ: The exact opposite  

So the goal here is to find the best possible balance. Or..  come up with a completely different system (to replace insurances).. Not so easy though..

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: Ah excellent, my proposal was more like this.   ===========  100,000 people insure their $1000 ships for $250 (<-- this is a lever) with the Universal DAO.  Coverage 100% (<--lever) but in ATLAS. Insurance pool $25,000,000. 10% of ships destroyed by pirates in the HRZ. Total compensation 10,000 ships x $1000/ship = $10,000,000. Amount made by pirates: $10m Amount made by DAO:  $15m Amount lost by normal players: $0 + deductible (the cost of the ships is technically higher, but that's because now the maximum loss you can incur on an insured ship is $250 making it a more stable asset) Amount made by normal players: whatever is brought into the universe by successfully completing their missions + the peace of mind and not tilting off the face of the earth when some asshat with 10 Fimbul Earps ruins their brand new Rainbow Arc.  Value created.  ===========  DAO gets rich. Pirates get rich. We get to cruise the black with peace of mind and without getting screwed. You can write incredibly complex insurance policies and change coverage and premium based on risk. If 50% of ships are attacked and destroyed in the HRZ by the other 50% then we can make premiums 50% if we cover both sides. We can pay out based on pool instead of inherent asset origination price, VWAP, cost to replace, etc. We can make something like a ship HSA where we hold the money to replace the asset in escrow until you get blown up or get back from the mission and keep the money we make in interest in the meantime.   ===========  The point is offering insurance from the DAO is a very lucrative proposition from the point of legitimate businessmen (aka competent pirates). Only testosterone bozos or people with scruples would go out there and steal in-person.   ** But here's the TL;DR: yoinking the NFT from the pirates takes all the fun out of it. If they wanted to make money they'd find a different job. They want the prize. The thrill. The booty. The cargo. Then they can go out and sell it and let fortune pay them for their "found" money. We may or may not want the ship back but if we're fanatics about the particular ship BUT we're serious about our actions having consequences, we shouldn't risking our paintjobs out in the black of space. We should stay close to home where the bad men can't get us. But we want the payouts from the HRZ you say? Ah, then we send ships we don't love that are insured. And we get our ATLAS back or we get the ATLAS from the mission. Everybody wins.

We may or may not want the ship back but if we're fanatics about the particular ship BUT we're serious about our actions having consequences, we shouldn't risking our paintjobs out in the black of space. We should stay close to home where the bad men can't get us. But we want the payouts from the HRZ you say? Ah, then we send ships we don't love that are insured. And we get our ATLAS back or we get the ATLAS from the mission. Everybody wins.

author: [AEP] MoonMonger
[AEP] MoonMonger
content:
I think there may be some pros and cons to inflationary, neutral or deflationary models that need complex modeling to understand the impact on the overall economy. Moreover, you need to layer that analysis with some insurable components being converted to raw materials (ex: ship, component, module) and some being straight NFT transfer (ex: crew, charm, loot)

Detailed outcomes and who gets how much of which loot whether under destruction or surrender (or even loss of internet connection!) would flow from this economic choice, but the choice itself isn't obvious and modeling can only be so accurate. In the end, I think we are still some years away from this and the team is on the right path with incremental releases, monitoring, analysis and remodeling, such as first safe zone, then MRZ, then HRZ releases where insurance would become relevant.

As far as the topic of 3rd party insurance, the answer is also not so clear. If insurance business is somehow profitable based on above modeling (as they are in real life, for example) then I can argue that I would rather have the SA DAO grow its treasury than a 3rd party who could be dumping profits on the open market.

author: outlawalpha5
content: Had a question for the group but might be more pertinent to only of us living in the US. Does anyone know if there is a plan to change how SA is handling the resources for ships and tracking as NFT's? I am going through my first tax filing with SA on my wallet and the transaction list is massive from the ship fleet module with supplies. I am concerned as most reporting systems cointracker, koinly, etc don't have these build into there systems yet and so no cost basis is being loaded for the sites so it is all manual. Each time I then supply each ship it creates a new string of transactions. Any thoughts would be appreciate and/or I could post in the Foundation Room but wanted to ask here first as I think it could be a barrier for US users that our filing.

author: 13omb3r
content: IMO y’all are asking the right questions- but your expectations are out of control. Yes an updated roadmap would be nice. Yes they should release better PR. But isn’t that why they have been on a hiring rampage? This team was founded about 2 years ago and everyone expects a mirror reality with cutting edge tech to be done yesterday. They have delivered more to us than any other video game has (while in production). Not saying that is everything- but it certainly gives me faith. I would also ask that you speaking for “all” of us- please don’t bunch us together with your thoughts. I am personally still very bullish on SA and have tons of faith in the team. Take a breathe people. I know it’s hard given the market, war, etc- but that is not SA’s problem however they are affected by it. Let’s just be realistic fam.  Yes updated roadmap = needed. Aside from that- let them take their time so the game isn’t destroyed before it ever releases. drops mic

author: Mjr_Eazy
content: I’ve ran multi-million dollar multi-year projects, biggest had >600 people on it and was a 5 year project, the key to delivery is ruthlessly manage scope and focus on the delivery now, this is where SA are going wrong IMHO.  Changing SCORE->MORE->CREAM->SCREAM!  Detail out the roadmap for next quarter (Now) put placeholders for what you expect in next (NEXT) quarter or 2 then everything is is just a list (LATER), they kinda do this but the key is then do what you said and if there’s delays explain why as sometimes things just go wrong/change - the dao issue is a classic example. You can clearly communicate what is being done & why and prioritize properly

author: JayWalk
content: GM SA community! Agreed to both of these. We need them to set expectations but not expect home run hits every time they are on a call. Let's find a way to help get the community to understand this is a marathon not a sprint. I think @SW4GN3R and his team have been great messaging that the whole journey is a game and should be enjoyed. We need to not focus on the destination of ROI but enjoy the ride and be supportive, this does come with some expectation from the team to be clearly communicating and making sure to not over set expectations. We all want to believe and the emotional roller coaster of not hitting deadlines might be more negative vs beneficial  as the community grows. I love the discussion around what is in the kitchen and able to be cooked, but most people it seems don't care as much about that but more about whatever comes out on the plate is delicious and look good.

author: JayWalk
content:
I know when people think DAO, they think POLIS staking, but what we are seeing with these ideas thrown out is a perfect example of what the DAO voting can do.  If it was up and running I would LOVE to see a vote on bringing on a full time scheduler with the responsibilities including: creating and updating a rolling wave schedule with a focus on current year tasking.  The schedule should be the foundational data used to update the roadmap, should be used to generate a high level GANTT chart and the scheduler should be responsible for updating the community on a monthly basis regarding current status including overall percent complete based upon predefined methodologies.  In addition the Scheduler should provide any variance analysis to the plan including delay impacts as well as root cause analysis of variances.

Imagine being able to look forward to that type of report on a monthly basis.

author: [HEIM] SDU pimpUstur
content: I am inclined to agree that is most likely confusing to a vast majority of people invested/following the project that deliverable names are constantly changing. We understand that for those watching closely, and for internal teams it probably all makes sense, but for 95% of people following the project it is confusing and can come across as Mjr_Eazy pointed out "that you are making it up as you go". I am sure we can all agree that we all want new people coming into the project, I believe that is the goal right? 1 million users by year end? In order for that to happen, alongside bringing in new people, the project needs to retain the interest of current users/players/investors. It will be hard for that to happen, as people seem to become disillusioned with the project's journey. I am inclined to agree that changing a deliverable name 3 times in 3 months does seem abit much, and can come across as a little "disorganized" to the onlooker. I don't know how hard it would be for each dept to write up a monthly report that can then be compiled and written out on some type of dev blog or something. doesn't have to be a 100 page long thesis, just maybe 3 or 4 key points from each department, outlining what was done that month, and what the plans are for next month. I understand these are probably all mentioned in TH, AMAs, and interviews, but having it written down someplace lends to its continuity and something we can refer to when people ask. I am confident this will help loads in regards to the question many of us have in regard to general comms about the project development.

author: [HEIM] SDU pimpUstur
content: And it's ok if things get scrapped, and need to go back to the drawing board, don't be afraid to tell us that there will be delays. I think most of us CORE users will back you up on that. I think I can speak for some people that we would rather you be blunt about delays, and why they are happening, and more honest about projected timelines, or needing to change projected timelines, over trying to meet the targets you set out, only to disappoint us when they are not met. I think most of us would back you up on this.

author: Lysimach | Make Polis Great [STAR]
content: I agree on project timelines, so far they all have been broken while saying something like "we're on track" until the last minute. I always prefer honesty -- say it like it is "I'm not sure we can deliver this by then because of this and that." And give a later date than trying to say we are aiming for a date when you know it's impossible. I would greatly appreciate if polis staking was said to be "we expect it to be done by July" and deliver it in May than to say we gonna deliver in February (you know you can't but you say it anyway) and then March (you know you can't but you say it anyway) and still deliver in May. We are hardcore backers here being so early and keep buying stuff, so I think we can handle the truth

author: ROME | Kalvin KideanONI
content:
⁠archived-foundation-room⁠
As much as I love Mass Effect and your first suggestion in any obsidian/bioware written game, I'm not convinced it would work here and it's hard to explain why. It's kind of like giving a kid a stick and a lightsaber. The stick stimulates imagination, risk, creativity, competition. The lightsaber sits on the shelf and looks pretty. This game is wildly open and we're creating our own stories in guilds and in these chat rooms. All they need to give us is a stick and we'll write stories on the blockchain. It takes a pretty linear story (like a movie) to make Mass Effect style relationships. Even when they gave you the option of who to flirt with it wrecked the complexity which is real relationships. And if Bioware can't do it no one can.

Also, judging from the trailer I'm not convinced this team can pull off lore or dialogue just yet. I'd hate to have conversation fails pulling me out of the universe every five minutes. 

I don't know if the devs ever show up in this room but I really want the crew to just be an extra layer of modules for the ship. They work for pay, they don't have COMMANDER loyalty (although you could give them Faction competence; i.e. pilots have experience with a certain kind of tech like Opal), and you can trade them to other fleet commanders (players) for ATLAS. Maybe you could do an extremely open kind of customization where we write their bios, but any kind of procedurally generated backstory will be wooden and pre-written lore would be hackneyed. Like orc commanders from Shadow of War. I'd be much more pleased if the devs gave us the tools to REALLY role-play with our own characters so we weren't just faceless avatars to the people we meet in social situations. I'm still very skeptical about why I would ever ride on a Jod Asteris cruise ship without a very robust chat experience.

I'm still very skeptical about why I would ever ride on a Jod Asteris cruise ship without a very robust chat experience.

reply.preview: ⁠archived-foundation-room⁠ As much as I love Mass Effect and your first suggestion in any obsidian/bioware written game, I'm not convinced it would work here and it's hard to explain why. It's kind of like giving a kid a stick and a lightsaber. The stick stimulates imagination, risk, creativity, competition. The lightsaber sits on the shelf and looks pretty. This game is wildly open and we're creating our own stories in guilds and in these chat rooms. All they need to give us is a stick and we'll write stories on the blockchain. It takes a pretty linear story (like a movie) to make Mass Effect style relationships. Even when they gave you the option of who to flirt with it wrecked the complexity which is real relationships. And if Bioware can't do it no one can. Also, judging from the trailer I'm not convinced this team can pull off lore or dialogue just yet. I'd hate to have conversation fails pulling me out of the universe every five minutes. I don't know if the devs ever show up in this room but I really want the crew to just be an extra layer of modules for the ship. They work for pay, they don't have COMMANDER loyalty (although you could give them Faction competence; i.e. pilots have experience with a certain kind of tech like Opal), and you can trade them to other fleet commanders (players) for ATLAS. Maybe you could do an extremely open kind of customization where we write their bios, but any kind of procedurally generated backstory will be wooden and pre-written lore would be hackneyed. Like orc commanders from Shadow of War. I'd be much more pleased if the devs gave us the tools to REALLY role-play with our own characters so we weren't just faceless avatars to the people we meet in social situations. I'm still very skeptical about why I would ever ride on a Jod Asteris cruise ship without a very robust chat experience.

author: ROME | Kalvin KideanONI
content: Sorry, maybe my suggestion wasn't entirely clear. I didn't mean to be exactly like ME, just to give an idea that crew members could be fun for some players, but for others could be disregarded (i.e. not really game-breaking if you don't like this feature). Rimworld has probably more details on that and it was what kept me playing the game, building my community of dudes and enjoying their hilarious interactions and events. Other players may had other goals and fun. So I'm just giving thoughts of what may be worthwhile exploring in the game.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content:
Trying to get caught up on some context, but I just wanted to make a couple of points here:

1. We absolutely want people from our team to able to participate in gameplay, including participation in DACs. A lot of our recent team recruits came directly from the community. Preventing our passionate users from participating in the product we are creating would be disproportionately unfair to that individual, and would eliminate a considerable portion of our potential talent pool.

2. We do have, and are continuing to refine our internal policies on both access to privileged information, and actions our employees can take on it. No one on the team is permitted to take action on material non-public information. While policing that is a challenge, I would note that we are collecting fairly extensive analytics through both our Marketing and Business Intelligence units. In addition to engaging with companies like Chainalysis - which I do want to comment is primarily to ensure we are in compliance with regulatory requirements - but can be utilized to analyze team member on-chain activity. It is also an area we will consistently improve through time. Violations of our policy would lead to immediate termination and forfeiture of long-term benefits in the company, including equity and token vesting.

3. Something I'll be addressing shortly in the Foundation Room is the concept of operations under Radical Transparency. As you are all aware, we attempt to share informational updates with the community regularly. It's a process we will continue improving upon. And while the minutia of every decision will not make it to the community, the lag between structural/material changes and the release of that information to the community should be limited. As a result, the window to capitalize on information asymmetry will be limited as well. 

There are no perfect systems here, and I'll repeat, we will continue to encounter challenges no teams have historically faced due to the disruptive nature of our product vision. However, I want to reassure all of you that we are considering how access to information impacts the community, and could disrupt game balance. @danparktica might care to comment further.

*edit: a final point - we do make every attempt to control access to information internally toonly* those individuals who need to have awareness of it, in an effort to prevent such information being abused. We presently do this through information based permissioned systems. Is it perfect? No. But again, we will continue improving upon these systems over time, working to prevent abuse by members on the team, and ensuring we are creating a fair environment for both our community and our team.

author: danparkticaONI
content:
@SW4GN3R hit all the main points.  I'll just emphasize that the team takes Star Atlas's reputation very seriously and wants to ensure the game is known for fair play and integrity in all respects.  We will not tolerate any abuse of the public's trust in our development work.  This means we will take swift action against any actual abuses of material nonpublic information, and also take steps to prevent even the appearance of impropriety.  As previously noted, it's critical that team members play the game so they understand how to make it as fun as possible.  It will be a balancing act, but we hope the community will come to appreciate our policy, especially as we start to explain the steps we are taking to monitor for, prevent, and punish any abuses of inside info.

For context, we have been putting a lot of work into improving our compliance processes in general, not just for inside info but also everything from IP infringement to international labor law requirements to anti-corruption laws and much more.  We are growing rapidly right now so we are building legal and compliance systems that we hope will scale well.

author: [AEP] Dez [AEP]
content: My thoughts on this are that there needs to be quite a bit of thought given to these ships before auction. Such as maybe limit of one per faction, they can't leave safe zone (at first?), will they be able to farm ATLAS in SCORE, what is the main purpose of this ship, etc... Nobody wants to see this is an unstoppable planet and fleet destroyer in game, I don't think...   Without that info I don't know who can spend, what like 500k-1M+, on a cat in the sack. Also, I believe one of the main purposes of launching these titan ships was to create a marketing buzz. I could argue now is NOT the best time to do it and moreover I don't see that time coming until we have SCREAM. The team doesn't need the money now anyway. Just thinking out loud of why it makes sense to launch titans after or with SCREAM.

author: Ghost [SKR]
content: Short squeeze.  Wagner posted that they would sell ATALS to pay a 1 time tax bill which resulted in a HUGE short interest in POLIS yesterday as shown by the lending APRs that went through the roof.  Today, with markets rallying (BTC and Stock Market) it put pressure on the shorts which forced them to cover their position resulting in ATLAS lending APRs to plummet to 19% right now.    All theory btw, but seems to fit the narrative.

This tweet contains no relevant data and no matter how much I love Star Atlas, I'm not going to push that tweet out to my followers.. I'd like to keep them after all

author: ROME | Kalvin KideanONI
content: Working on one that meets some of that criteria.  Guild will focus on Photoli technology with the idea of creating a centralized marketplace (or 3 markets per faction) where good will be sold.  Will be Faction agnostic and will have loyalty rewards.  Prefer to not fight in HRZ, but rather access Photoli by utilizing Stealth and Radar tech and avoid conflict wherever possible (although a need for security will most likely be needed).  Also will obviously need a resource division to acquire necessary resources for manufacturing.  Could also be scaled up to include Tufa tech possibly too.

author: odvb
content: Yes, this is going to happen, just as it does in any game. But I am sure there will be many disclaimers in TOS, and everyone just has to be aware of that possibility, and decide their own risk tolerance accordingly. But I am confident that unlike other games, where exploits remain un-patched for months, exploits found in Star Atlas may last a day or two at most, before getting fixed, precisely because of the fact that real $$$ is at stake.

author: ROME | KING SHRED [STAR]
content: regarding "transporting players and their assets" point you bring up in foundation room... When I think about this, it actually introduced a bunch more complexity and questions. First is the insurance if you are going to HRZ on transport. Will each player need their own insurance then, even if they are not flying in with their own ship?  Then there is the assets. If they travel on transport, do they become a crew member NFT or is "player" a non-NFT persona? Also if assets are brought, then I suppose there would need to be room for them on the transport.  Finally, if you can bring assets means that if you travel somewhere without them, does that mean you can't use them even if they are in your wallet because you didn't pack them on the trip? That also brings up the need for a home base where your assets are parked and whether that is a concept at all.

reply.preview: regarding "transporting players and their assets" point you bring up in foundation room... When I think about this, it actually introduced a bunch more complexity and questions. First is the insurance if you are going to HRZ on transport. Will each player need their own insurance then, even if they are not flying in with their own ship? Then there is the assets. If they travel on transport, do they become a crew member NFT or is "player" a non-NFT persona? Also if assets are brought, then I suppose there would need to be room for them on the transport. Finally, if you can bring assets means that if you travel somewhere without them, does that mean you can't use them even if they are in your wallet because you didn't pack them on the trip? That also brings up the need for a home base where your assets are parked and whether that is a concept at all.

author: ROME | Kalvin KideanONI
content:
All of this seems to be related to the metadata they keep talking about to mark our locations. We can't transfer reagents and modules from wallet to wallet and change where they are in verse. It would make sense that we'd have to transport crew members using a different method than freight but the same rules would apply. 

If @ROME | KING SHRED  is just talking about crew members being transported via lowbie/jod asteris/whatever, I think they've been clear enough that those people can be captured/put at risk/killed, and they'll need to employ a security detail/convoy. But what about us? If I take a 1 man fighter out into the black does that limit my exposure to risk?

I think to clarify the real question is what is your player character in game? Can you play through any of your personnel's eyes? Are you a factional force that leads your fleet from the control room? If you are a specific character (think most of the intel has lead us in that direction) are you unkillable? Uncapturable? If you're taken hostage or blown up in the HRZ where do you wake up and why? 

Anyway, good questions all.

author: Lysimach | Make Polis Great [STAR]
content: "Ubisoft Scalar is a new foundational technology that enables Ubisoft titles to utilize the power of the cloud to ensure that developers aren’t limited by time or hardware, but by their imaginations. The result could enable game worlds that are bigger, more social, and richer than anything that’s ever been seen before at Ubisoft."

author: Lysimach | Make Polis Great [STAR]
content: I guess the tools are becoming available as the time goes by

author: Lysimach | Make Polis Great [STAR]
content: "Ubisoft Scalar is a production tool that opens up the possibilities of cloud for our games, removing that traditional barrier of the local hardware you have at home and widening the field of what types of games can be created. It is a tool to open up new possibilities for creatives, and due to that I expect to see a broader spectrum of games coming out of it in the future.

author: Lysimach | Make Polis Great [STAR]
content: Developers can create very rich worlds that can develop, evolve, and change as players are playing them. It brings a much closer connection to developers and players.

author: Lysimach | Make Polis Great [STAR]
content: It will become possible to have more players in a single shared world, which I think will introduce new social experiences that we haven’t seen before. You can also create world simulations on a scale and to a complexity we’ve never seen before."

author: [HEIM] SDU pimpUstur
content: I find it absolutely imperative as founding members of this great game that when we purchase a ship that the origination components/modules stay intact and that any changes to ships are made globally known. author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content:
Good day everyone!

I want to remind everybody that this channel is meant to discuss messages posted in the ⁠archived-foundation-room. It's not meant as a replacement for ⁠👽┃general.

In fact, the off-topic messages posted this weekend are exactly the type of messages that would do ⁠👽┃general good at any given day. So please consider move these discussions there  

Let's not turn this channel into a VIP-general room. I agree that it is tempting, being among a segment of very passionate SA enthusiasts, but it could actually potentially hurt the overall image of Star Atlas. If the public channels only contain the less-knowledgeable, less joyous banter... Well, it's the first impression newcomers to our wonderful community get  

Thanks for reading!

content:
It would be good if anyone could read these channels/chats. Maybe make the boost that only gives right to talk. Because I think if everyone knows how many reply you guys do on community questions, remarks or compliment I think that's the only marketing you need to gain momentum that you guys want. I would say that getting 1 mil players in and keeping them occupied for 5 years will be a enormous task.

author: [AEP] MoonMonger
content:
IMO, a balanced and sustainable economy is critical to the success of Star Atlas and user growth. This carries through to in-game assets being balanced as well. It is impossible for SA team to achieve perfect balance out of the gate without seeing how the assets will be used in game, especially given the complexity of the vision and all the factors at play.

We should fully expect this balancing with nerfs and buffs to assets to be an iterative and consistent process. It will be in the interest of the balanced economy and hence our interests. The best we can do now and going forward is expect it to happen and manage risk by diversification of assets.

author: Lysimach | Make Polis Great [STAR]
content: I disagree with this expectation. The stability of the economy rests on receiving a certain amount of utility related to the size of your investment. Once we have the asset in hand it should not be changeable by the dev team. These ship "skeletons" as it were are changeable because we've basically just bought participation tickets at this point, but once we've built a module with certain stats costing "x" amount of time and money they should not be able to regress our technology. This is the antithesis of the only place where that flies, a centralized economy. Balance might be introduced in the form of progressive technology. They could try and catch the unbalanced mistake. Or the community could hunt down that mistake and remove it from the game. But I think if you find the arbitrage opportunity to pay 10k for a 50k weapon no one should be able to take it from you. The market price will adjust to its level of power. You cannot take that power from the people.

author: ROME | Kalvin KideanONI
content:
@Lysimach | Make Polis Great It's not pointless. It's a philosophy which will inform the rest of the game and that we need to know. Are our assets ours, unequal (not unfair) or not? The difference between a game and the metaverse is a connection with reality. These ships represent risks and sacrifices on our part and before we make more risks and sacrifices we should know whether or not we own these assets to do what we want with with or without meddling and the extent to which they can be meddled with. That's private property. Was it unfair that most of you got lucky in ETH? Was it an overpowered protocol? Should it have been nerfed to equal out the chances for the other people buying crypto? People get lucky. That's reality and if we don't reflect that in this game that's supposed to be "meta" then what the hell are we all doing here? 

@[HEIM] SDU pimp I respect these three opinions. And I agree with the unrelated proposal that "game-breaking mechanics" should be fixed so the game isn't broken.   I also agree that if private property rights are violated it should be at the hands of the investors/citizenry instead of the designers. Out of all the bad options that one is the least egregious.

author: bunthius [STAR]
content: backstory on that 2min video. it wasn't ever meant to be showcased publicly. it was more of an internal show and tell. i recorded it just using windows video recorder directly in engine. so there is no optimizations there at all. that recorder drops the framerate a lot so the unit stats are useless. and those frame rate skips are in the original video. also i have 2 4k ultrawides and 2 1440p monitors on the same card also multitasking with multiple other windows hogging resources. so anyway the quality would have been much more streamlined had it been a planning public showcase/marketing release. sorry about that. there is a higher quality raw video but you just get more pixels the skipping is still there. also the showroom hasn't been put through performance optimizations yet and so a lot will be sped up there as well.

author: [HEIM] SDU pimpUstur
content:
In response to ⁠archived-foundation-room⁠
I mean, it's hard to say how things will pan out, but imagine that something needs a nerf, most likely that ship/item will have accrued value through demand due to it being OP. All the nerf would do is take it back down to its more true market valuation.  You have some options there as the owner of said asset, when you notice it is accruing value because of the OP stat or ability, you can either sell or be aware that if you don't sell, most likely it will get nerfed, and valuation will go back down. I really don't think it's that big of a deal, tbh
And of course, on the other party, the party that buys an OP item, and is not following the reasons why it may be nerfed, and then gets burned, well that's on them for not having done the research beforehand

reply.preview: In response to ⁠archived-foundation-room⁠ I mean, it's hard to say how things will pan out, but imagine that something needs a nerf, most likely that ship/item will have accrued value through demand due to it being OP. All the nerf would do is take it back down to its more true market valuation. You have some options there as the owner of said asset, when you notice it is accruing value because of the OP stat or ability, you can either sell or be aware that if you don't sell, most likely it will get nerfed, and valuation will go back down. I really don't think it's that big of a deal, tbh And of course, on the other party, the party that buys an OP item, and is not following the reasons why it may be nerfed, and then gets burned, well that's on them for not having done the research beforehand

author: 13omb3r
content: At the end of the day, the ship's performance will be a culmination of hull, crew, modules, components, and maybe other things added later. if a certain module become OP, and everyone starts putting it on their ships, or starts selling that module, and it gets nerfed, the integrity of your ship remains intact, it is merely the module that would get a nerf. that is my understanding. of course, the downsizing and/or upsizing of module and component slots on your ships, i'm guessing that is where your concern comes in. in which case, it would have to be done properly so as to avoid confusion, imagine you have your ship kitted out with 1 large missile bay, and you've levelled it up bigtime, and all of a sudden you have 2 medium missile bays. as a resolution, they could make it that along with the nerf, or whatever, they would also replace your 1 large missile bay upgraded to the same upgraded 2x medium missile bays, if that makes sense.

author: Redspatula.io/star-atlas-shipsUstur
content:
Made a fleet page to check out any fleet with a Solana public address (First version). 
https://redspatula.io/star-atlas-fleet/

Biggest update we have ever released so far. 
You can enter a public Solana address that's staking ships to see a breakdown of Rewards and Emissions

Some of the features to mention:
1. Amount of resources each fleet burns a day, month, year
2. Amount of resources cost in Atlas per day per fleet, also converted into current USDC price
3. Amount of rewards earned (Gross & Net) in a day, month year, In Atlas and also converted into current USDC price.
4. Total Atlas Rewards claimed since Enlistment.
5. Share fleet URL with friends to show off your fleet. 

Here is a link to a demo account if you don't have your own fleet yet to try it: https://redspatula.io/star-atlas-fleet/AjmkWjndEfSVYaYJk94uWdBMa9b3HUePLCJgsjdQPvsQ

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: VIP Badges provide a special parking spot at the CSS as well as expedited entry into the station (Priority Lines).   It sounds like they intend for these to be highly congested with ships potentially having to queue for docking and entrants going through some type of checkpoint.   Badges have also been said to come with other (unannounced perks) which may include early-access to other events and/or airdrops.Permit is not a Badge.
